key_insight: The paper verifies a sequential, non-iterative fixed-stress thermo-poroelastic coupling in PFLOTRAN that solves flow/thermal fields first and mechanics second, with results matching benchmark solutions.
engineering_relevance: This provides a validated THM modeling foundation for predicting coupled pressure, temperature, and deformation in subsurface systems such as enhanced geothermal energy and underground storage.
method: Implicit finite-volume TH solve for pressure and temperature on control-volume/Voronoi cells
Quasi-static mechanics solve on an element-based dual mesh
Sequential fixed-stress split with flow-mechanics mapping, including fracture/discontinuity treatment
evidence_signal: The implementation is assessed against poroelastic and thermo-poroelastic benchmarks, showing agreement with analytical or semi-analytical solutions for pressure diffusion, temperature evolution, and deformation.
watch_item: Because the approach is strictly sequential and non-iterative, accuracy and robustness may depend on coupling strength, mesh mapping quality, and discontinuity representation.
paper_title: Verification of a sequential thermo-poroelasticity formulation in PFLOTRAN
